# Jesomma Formation

**Summary**: A sedimentary aquifer primarily composed of sandstone with some limestone.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1993-moallim|Moallim, 1993]] evaluated the Jesomma formation as a highly transmissive and confined aquifer.

## Depositional Environment

- [[1993-moallim|Moallim, 1993]] fluvial environment

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The average transmissivity of the Jesomma aquifer is about 3.11 x 10^7 m^2/sec. ([[1993-moallim|Moallim, 1993, p. 114]])
